cards.title: VLM-IE3D: Injecting Implicit and Explicit 3D Geometry Tokens into VLMs via RGB Video
cards.why_it_matters: For researchers working on spatial reasoning or multimodal agents, this is a clean architecture for 3D-aware VLMs that requires no depth sensors or 3D inputs — only RGB video.
cards.summary: VLM-IE3D augments VLMs with two geometry token types: Implicit Geometry Tokens (IGTs) capturing high-level geometric priors from video, and Explicit Geometry Tokens (EGTs) encoding detailed structure from reconstructed 3D attributes. A 3D-aware adapter fuses both with 2D visual features. The RGB-only design improves performance on 3D video detection, visual grounding, dense captioning, and spatial reasoning. Code and models are public.

cards.title: Expanding Flow Maps (EFMs): Flow-based Generative Models with Variable Output Dimensionality
cards.why_it_matters: For researchers building generative models over graphs or variable-length sequences, EFMs offer a principled framework where output size is itself a learned degree of freedom rather than fixed at design time.
cards.summary: EFlows define flows between distributions of increasing dimensionality via an expanding interpolant that augments state with conditional noise. EFMs distill this into few-step models by factoring each timestep transition into a learnable expand operator (adds coordinates/tokens conditioned on current state) and a transport map. Fixed-canvas flows are recovered as the special case where expand is identity. The framework extends to the discrete simplex, enabling variable-size graph and variable-length sequence generation.

cards.title: GraphVid: Graph-Conditioned Image-to-Video Generation Cuts FID by 39.9% and FVD by 37.6% vs Motion-I2V
cards.why_it_matters: For researchers in controllable generation, structured graph interfaces as control signals are a compelling alternative to trajectory drawing — especially for complex multi-object scenes.
cards.summary: GraphVid conditions image-to-video generation on structured interaction graphs rather than per-object pixel trajectories, enabling flexible multi-subject control. A large-scale dataset GraphVid-Bench provides interaction-centric video with relational annotations. Compared to Motion-I2V, GraphVid reduces FID by up to 39.9% and FVD by 37.6%, and improves PSNR from 9.87 to 15.98 and SSIM from 0.38 to 0.61, using less training data and fewer trainable parameters.

cards.title: Nunchaku 4-bit W4A4 Diffusion Inference Now Native in Diffusers
cards.why_it_matters: If you run diffusion model inference at scale, this collapses the barrier to W4A4 quantization — no custom engine, no local CUDA compilation, just from_pretrained().
cards.summary: SVDQuant's Nunchaku quantization (W4A4 via outlier-absorbed low-rank residual branches) is now loadable directly in Diffusers via the kernels package. NVFP4 checkpoints on RTX 5090 hit ~1.7s for 1024×1024 images at ~12 GB peak vs ~24 GB for BF16. A diffuse-compressor toolkit lets you quantize and publish your own architectures. NVFP4 requires Blackwell (RTX 50xx); INT4 variants cover earlier GPUs.

cards.title: QST-Flow: Normalizing Flows for Scalable Continuous-Variable Quantum State Tomography
cards.why_it_matters: For researchers at the intersection of quantum ML and quantum information, this offers a neural density approach to tomography that avoids fixed-grid truncation and handles sign-changing Wigner functions — a known hard problem.
cards.summary: QST-Flow represents phase-space quasiprobability distributions as neural densities via normalizing flows. QST-QFlow models the positive Husimi-Q with a single flow; QST-WFlow handles sign-changing Wigner functions as a trainable difference of two normalized flows. The approach preserves normalization, enables exact density evaluation and direct sampling, and benchmarks favorably on cat, binomial, GKP, number, and Fock states with improved reconstruction error over prior ML tomography methods.

cards.title: Barzilai-Borwein Optimizer Cannot Converge Superlinearly on a Positive-Measure Set of Quadratics (n≥4)
cards.why_it_matters: For ML engineers who rely on BB-style adaptive gradient methods, this result closes a long-open theoretical question with a concrete negative answer backed by computer-assisted proof.
cards.summary: The paper proves that for every dimension n≥4, there exists a nonempty open (positive Lebesgue measure) family of strictly convex quadratic problems and initializations for which BB1 converges but cannot converge root-superlinearly. Explicit constants ρ_min=10⁻⁶ and ρ_max=0.61 bound gradient spectral components above and below by geometric sequences. The construction uses a computer-assisted proof of a nonresonant attracting seven-cycle in the projectivized BB dynamics in dimension four.
